senses.definition: a person or thing that accompanies or is added to something else, especially a sidecar passenger or a secondary feature
examples: The motorcycle had a trained sidecarist who helped with luggage.
In the exhibit, the artist played the role of a sidecarist, adding a supplementary element to the performance.

senses.definition: plural of sidecar, a framework attached to the side of a vehicle carrying a passenger or cargo
examples: The motorcycle had two sidecars for extra passengers.
They attached two sidecars to the bike for a family trip.

senses.definition: a chair placed at the side of a main chair, often used for assistants or attendants; can refer to a secondary seat in a conference or ceremony
examples: The speaker sat in the sidechair beside the chairperson.
A sidechair was brought for the guest of honor.

senses.definition: plural of sidechair; auxiliary chairs placed to the side of a main seating arrangement
examples: The dining room had two sidechairs for extra guests.
During the ceremony, sidechairs were arranged along the wall.

senses.definition: a check or inspection performed on the side or flank of a device, vehicle, or structure; also used metaphorically for a secondary review
examples: Perform a sidecheck of the chassis before the race.
A quick sidecheck revealed no leaks.

senses.definition: plural of sidecheck; multiple secondary inspections
examples: The engineer performed several sidechecks before departure.
Sidechecks ensured there were no hidden faults.

senses.definition: pliers with insulated handles used for cutting wire or small metal pieces at the side of a work area
examples: He used sidecutters to trim the copper wire.
The knife was too dull; he grabbed the sidecutters instead.

senses.definition: having a specified side or sides; oriented toward a side
examples: The sided arrow points to the left.
A sided window pane has two visible faces.

senses.definition: the quality or condition of having sides or being biased toward a side
examples: The debate revealed the sidedness of the crowd.
Sidedness in the map caused misinterpretation of territory.

senses.definition: plural of sidedness
examples: Different experiments showed varying sidednesses in perception.
Researchers noted many sidednesses across cultures.

senses.definition: to apply fertilizer to the sides of row crops during cultivation
examples: Farmers sidedress the corn to boost growth.
They sidedressed the field after the first rain.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of sidedress; applies fertilizer to the sides of crops
examples: The farmer sidedresses the beans after planting.
They sidedresses fields to improve yield.

senses.definition: a light positioned to the side of a subject to create depth or drama in photography or cinematography
examples: The photographer used a sideflash to highlight the model's cheekbone.
A sideflash helped separate the subject from the background.

senses.definition: a heading placed on the side of a page or article; a marginal note
examples: The sidehead indicated the next section.
In magazine layout, sideheads help guide readers.

senses.definition: a hill on the side of which something is located or a road that ascends the side of a hill
examples: They drove up the steep sidehill road.
Pasture on the sidehill is prone to erosion.

senses.definition: plural of sidehill; multiple side slopes or hillsides
examples: The hikers climbed the sidehills to reach the crest.
Fences were built along the sidehills to prevent landslides.

senses.definition: a holding or grip on the side of something; a side hold in climbing or mechanics
examples: The climber found a secure sidehold on the rock face.
A proper sidehold kept the wheel steady during the turn.

senses.definition: a close friend or partner who accompanies another person, often in adventures or routines
examples: The hero's loyal sidekick helped in every mission.
In old cartoons, the sidekick was always by the main character's side.

senses.definition: a person who frequently accompanies another, often in a casual or informal sense; a sidekick
examples: The comic's sidekickers kept the audience laughing.
She introduced her sidekicker to the audience.

senses.definition: plural of sidekick; close companions who accompany a main character
examples: The heroes' sidekicks joined the quest.
Many stories feature loyal sidekicks who provide humor and help.

senses.definition: language used in the margin or alongside main text; a concept in linguistics or typesetting
examples: The sidelang notes clarified the main text.
Practice with sidelang can help comprehension.

senses.definition: having no side or lacking a side; entirely open or bare on the side
examples: The sideless wheel showed the axle clearly.
A sideless cloak left the shoulder bare.

senses.definition: levers positioned on the side of a machine for control or adjustment
examples: Adjust the machine with the sidelevers.
The operator used sidelevers to fine-tune the settings.

senses.definition: a windowed or lit feature on the side of a building or a light located at the side of a vehicle or stage
examples: The house has a glass sidelight by the door.
The car’s sidelight helped illuminate the side street.

senses.definition: plural of sidelight; windows or lights on the side of a building or stage lighting
examples: The foyer has sidelights to brighten the entrance.
The stage used sidelights to frame the actors.
